# SnapTray — Full Documentation

> Powerful screenshot and recording tool that lives in your system tray.

SnapTray is a free, native desktop application for macOS and Windows built with Qt 6. It provides region capture, on-screen annotation, pin windows, screen canvas mode, MP4 recording with optional audio capture, and GIF/WebP export.

## Platform Support

- macOS 14+ (Apple Silicon and Intel)
- Windows 10+ (x64)

## Links

- Website: https://snaptray.cc
- Documentation: https://snaptray.cc/docs/
- Getting Started: https://snaptray.cc/docs/getting-started/
- FAQ: https://snaptray.cc/docs/faq/
- Release Notes: https://snaptray.cc/releases/
- Privacy Policy: https://snaptray.cc/privacy/
- Source Code: https://github.com/victorfu/snap-tray

---

## Getting Started

### What you will complete in 10 minutes

- Permission setup
- First screenshot and annotation
- First pin window
- First recording export
- Hotkey conflict check

### Runtime prerequisites

#### macOS

- macOS 14+
- Screen Recording permission
- Accessibility permission recommended for window detection and smoother workflows

#### Windows

- Windows 10+
- Microphone permission only when recording audio

### Permission checklist

#### macOS

Before your first serious capture session, verify:

1. `System Settings > Privacy & Security > Screen Recording`
2. `System Settings > Privacy & Security > Accessibility`

Enable SnapTray in both places, then restart the app if you changed permissions.

#### Windows

Most runtime permissions are handled by the OS and installer shape. If you record audio, confirm the selected microphone or audio source is available and permitted.

### Step 1: Verify tray runtime (1 min)

1. Launch SnapTray.
2. Confirm the tray icon is visible.
3. Open the menu and verify these entries exist: Region Capture, Screen Canvas, Settings.

### Step 2: Test screenshot flow (2 min)

1. Press `F2`.
2. Drag any region and confirm the magnifier and size overlay appear.
3. Add one arrow annotation.
4. Press `Ctrl/Cmd + C` and paste into a text editor.
5. Press `Ctrl/Cmd + S` and confirm the file was written.

### Step 3: Test pin workflow (2 min)

1. Capture again with `F2`.
2. Press `Enter` to pin the result.
3. Move, resize, and zoom the pin window.
4. Switch apps and confirm the pin stays visible.

### Step 4: Test recording flow (3 min)

1. Start recording from the tray menu or press `R` from the capture toolbar.
2. Record 5 to 10 seconds.
3. Stop and export MP4.
4. Confirm the output path in Settings > Files.

### Step 5: Resolve hotkey conflicts (2 min)

Open Settings > Hotkeys and rebind any conflicting actions.

---

## Region Capture

Press `F2` (default) to enter Region Capture. The cursor switches to crosshair, and the cursor companion appears.

### Selection behavior

- Magnifier mode provides pixel-level feedback
- Width and height are shown near the selection
- Hold `Shift` to constrain aspect ratio
- Window detection allows one-click window selection
- Right-click or `Esc` to cancel

### Multi-region workflow

Press `M` to toggle multi-region capture mode for collecting multiple regions.

### Post-selection actions

- Annotation tools (Arrow/Pencil/Marker/Shape/Text/Mosaic/Step Badge/Emoji/Eraser)
- OCR and QR Code Scan
- Auto Blur (faces + credentials)
- Share URL
- Pin, Save, Copy, and Record

### Shortcuts

| Action | Shortcut |
|---|---|
| Start Region Capture | `F2` |
| Copy color code | `C` |
| Constrain ratio | Hold `Shift` |
| Toggle multi-region | `M` |
| Start recording | `R` |
| Cancel | `Esc` |

---

## Annotation Tools

### Tool categories

#### Drawing

- Pencil
- Marker
- Arrow (single-line and polyline modes)
- Shape (rectangle/ellipse)

#### Markup and guidance

- Text
- Step Badge
- Emoji Sticker

#### Privacy and correction

- Mosaic
- Eraser

### Editing workflow

1. Draw rough annotations first.
2. Adjust color and width globally.
3. Add text and step badges for sequence.
4. Apply mosaic to sensitive areas.
5. Use Undo/Redo before export.

### Export actions

- `Enter` to pin result
- `Ctrl/Cmd + C` to copy
- `Ctrl/Cmd + S` to save

---

## Pin Window

### Create a pin window

Pin from capture result (`Enter`) or from tray menu using "Pin from Image...".

### Mouse actions

- Drag to move
- Mouse wheel to zoom
- `Ctrl` + wheel to adjust opacity
- Drag edge to resize

### Keyboard actions

| Action | Key |
|---|---|
| Rotate clockwise | `1` |
| Rotate counter-clockwise | `2` |
| Flip horizontal | `3` |
| Flip vertical | `4` |
| Close | `Esc` |

### Context menu actions

- View/layout: Show Toolbar, Show Border, Move to Screen, Merge Pins, Adjust Region Layout
- Image controls: Zoom presets, image processing, smoothing
- Output/workflow: Copy, Save, Open Cache Folder, Beautify, Watermark, Live Update
- Interaction/close: Click-through, Close, Close All Pins

### Annotation in pin window

Drawing tools: Pencil, Marker, Arrow, Shape, Text, Mosaic, Step Badge, Emoji, Eraser
Utility tools: Crop, Measure
Action tools: OCR, QR Code Scan, Share URL, Save, Copy

---

## Screen Canvas

Press `Ctrl+F2 / Cmd+F2` (default) or use tray menu "Screen Canvas".

### Available tools

- Pencil / Marker / Arrow / Shape
- Text / Step Badge / Emoji
- Laser Pointer for presentation

### Background modes

- Whiteboard: Best for dark room projection or clean note visuals
- Blackboard: Better contrast when highlighting bright colors

### Session controls

- Undo / Redo
- Clear canvas
- `Esc` to exit

---

## Recording

### Entry points

- Tray menu: Record Full Screen
- Region capture toolbar: press `R`

### Recording lifecycle

1. Pick region or full-screen target.
2. Click Start.
3. Use floating control bar to Pause/Resume.
4. Click Stop to export.

### Output formats

| Format | Typical use |
|---|---|
| MP4 (H.264) | Tutorials, long recordings |
| GIF | Short looping demos |
| WebP | Lightweight animated snippets |

### Audio options (MP4 only)

- Microphone
- System audio
- Mixed capture

GIF and WebP exports are silent.

---

## Hotkeys

### Default hotkeys

| Action | Default |
|---|---|
| Region Capture | `F2` |
| Screen Canvas | `Ctrl+F2 / Cmd+F2` |
| Paste | `F3` |
| Quick Pin | `Shift+F2` |

### Additional configurable actions (no default)

- Pin from Image
- Pin History
- Toggle Hide/Show All Pins
- Record Full Screen

### Hotkey categories

Capture, Canvas, Clipboard, Pin, Recording

---

## CLI

The CLI is SnapTray's official automation interface for scripting capture workflows, IPC control, and settings management.

### Install CLI helper

- macOS: Settings > General > CLI install (creates `/usr/local/bin/snaptray`)
- Windows: Settings > General > CLI install (adds to PATH)

### Command matrix

| Command | Description | Requires main instance |
|---|---|---|
| `full` | Capture full screen | No |
| `screen` | Capture specific screen | No |
| `region` | Capture region | No |
| `gui` | Open region capture GUI | Yes |
| `canvas` | Toggle Screen Canvas | Yes |
| `record` | Start/stop/toggle recording | Yes |
| `pin` | Pin an image file or clipboard | Yes |
| `config` | Manage settings | Partial |

### Example commands

```bash
snaptray full                         # Capture screen under cursor
snaptray full -c                      # Copy to clipboard
snaptray full -d 1000 -o shot.png     # Delay 1s, save
snaptray screen --list                # List screens
snaptray region -r 0,0,800,600 -c     # Capture region to clipboard
snaptray gui                          # Open region selector
snaptray canvas                       # Toggle Screen Canvas
snaptray record start                 # Start recording
snaptray record stop                  # Stop recording
snaptray pin -f image.png             # Pin image file
snaptray pin -c --center              # Pin clipboard centered
snaptray config --list                # List settings
snaptray config --set files/filenamePrefix SnapTray
```

### Return codes

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `0` | Success |
| `1` | General error |
| `2` | Invalid arguments |
| `3` | File error |
| `4` | Instance error |

---

## Settings

### Tabs

- **General**: Launch at startup, language, theme, CLI install
- **Hotkeys**: Centralized hotkey editor by category
- **Advanced**: Magnifier, blur, pin defaults, MCP toggle
- **Watermark**: Image, opacity, scale, margin, position
- **OCR**: Language order, post-recognition behavior
- **Recording**: Frame rate, format, preview, audio, countdown
- **Files**: Screenshot/recording folders, filename templates
- **Updates**: Version, auto-check interval, manual check
- **About**: App info, version, copyright

---

## FAQ

### What is SnapTray?

SnapTray is a free, lightweight screenshot and screen recording tool for macOS and Windows. It lives in your system tray and lets you capture regions, annotate screenshots, pin images, record your screen to MP4/GIF/WebP, and share results instantly.

### Is SnapTray free?

Yes, SnapTray is completely free and open source under the MIT license. There are no ads, subscriptions, or hidden costs.

### How is SnapTray different from Snipping Tool?

SnapTray goes beyond basic screen capture by offering on-screen annotation tools, pin windows for always-on-top references, screen canvas mode, MP4 recording with audio, GIF/WebP export, OCR text extraction, auto blur for sensitive content, QR code scanning, and a full CLI for automation.

### Does SnapTray upload my captures?

No automatic upload happens by default. Everything stays local unless you explicitly use the Share URL action.

### Which output format should I use?

- MP4 for long videos and tutorials
- GIF for short loops or small demos
- WebP for lighter animated snippets

### Can I use SnapTray from scripts?

Yes. The CLI is the official automation interface. See the CLI section above for the complete command reference.

### Where should I report bugs?

Open an issue on GitHub with OS version, SnapTray version, reproducible steps, and screenshots or recordings when relevant.

---

## Troubleshooting

### Capture does not start

- macOS: Verify Screen Recording and Accessibility permissions, restart after changes
- Windows: Update GPU drivers, confirm runtime dependencies

### Tray icon does not appear

- Relaunch SnapTray
- Confirm no OS security prompts blocked the app

### Hotkey not responding

- Check Settings > Hotkeys for conflicts
- Rebind and test immediately

### Recording issues

- Lower frame rate for dropped frames
- Prefer MP4 for longer recordings
- macOS system audio requires macOS 13+ or BlackHole
